description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The stone appears to be a dense, speckled gray material, likely granite or concrete, with a slightly rough texture. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. This type of block is commonly used as a tactile paving or barrier in urban environments to guide or restrict pedestrian movement.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, made of speckled gray stone and featuring a robust, solid form.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal groove pattern across the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical alignment of the grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface wear and slight discoloration visible, likely from foot traffic and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with adjacent paving or curb elements
